title: Wire CLI execute path to interpreter
purpose: The CLI currently loads a .beam file, resolves imports, and then returns ExecutionUnavailable. The interpreter exists and works. This brief wires the last mile: creating a Process, setting its code pointer to the resolved entry function, calling interpreter::run_with_registry(), and returning the result.
task: Replace the Err(CliError::ExecutionUnavailable) return in execute_beam() with actual execution. Create a Process with a fresh heap, set its code pointer to the resolved CodePointer, populate X-registers with CLI-provided arguments, call interpreter::run_with_registry(), and handle the ExecutionResult.
boundaries: This brief SHALL NOT implement multi-module loading (one .beam file per invocation)
This brief SHALL NOT implement a REPL or interactive mode
This brief SHALL NOT implement scheduler-based execution (single-process, single-threaded run loop)

requirements.title: Wire execute_beam to interpreter
requirements.spec: execute_beam() SHALL create a Process with Process::new(pid, DEFAULT_HEAP_SIZE), set its code position to the resolved CodePointer (module, label), and call interpreter::run_with_registry(). The function SHALL return Ok(()) on normal exit and Err(CliError::Exec(...)) on error.
acceptance: cargo run -- tests/fixtures/proof.beam --entry proof:answer/0 prints the return value and exits 0
cargo run -- tests/fixtures/proof.beam --entry proof:add/2 -- 17 25 prints 42 and exits 0
A module with no matching export prints an error and exits 1
The ExecutionUnavailable variant is removed from CliError

requirements.title: Print execution result
requirements.spec: On normal exit, the CLI SHALL print the value in x(0) to stdout using the term display format. On error exit, the CLI SHALL print the exit reason to stderr. Exit code SHALL be 0 for normal termination and 1 for any error.
acceptance: Normal exit prints the return value to stdout
Error exit prints the reason to stderr
Exit code 0 on normal, 1 on error

requirements.title: CLI argument passing
requirements.spec: When the user provides arguments after --, the CLI SHALL parse them as terms and load them into X-registers before execution. Integer literals SHALL be parsed as small integers. Atom literals SHALL be interned and loaded as atom terms. Arity mismatch SHALL print an error and exit 1.
acceptance: -- 17 25 loads x(0)=17, x(1)=25 for an arity-2 function
-- hello loads x(0)=atom(hello) for an arity-1 function
Arity mismatch prints a clear error message
No arguments works for arity-0 functions
